T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H 
115
E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Stomach Meridian Neck Muscles 7-9AM
YANG
B MUSCLE ORIGIN AND INSERTION Anterior Neck Flexors Insertion Origin: First third of the collarbone  and top 
of the breastbone (Sternum). Anterior 
Neck Flexors Insertion: Mastoid bone at the back of the 
head, behind the ears. Origin NEUROLYMPHATIC 
MASSAGE POINTS 2-3 Feel these muscles contracting in the front 
and anterior sides of your neck as you bring 
your head forward. Front: 3 1/2“ from the 
sternum, on and between 	
2nd and 3rd ribs. Posterior Neck Extensors Back:  At C2, near the top of 
the neck, about 3/4” to each 
side of the spine. Origin: From the lowest cervical vertebra 
(C7) through the upper back (T1-5) on the 
Spinus and Transverse Processes. Insertion: At the entire posterior base of 
the skull just above the spine. SPINAL 
REFLEX
POINT NOTE FACIAL EXPRESSIONS: Feel this muscle contracting at the back 
of your neck and the posterior sides of your 
neck as you bring your head back. There are so many muscles involved in the 
neck that it is a very subtle practice trying 
to isolate, sense and test any individual 
muscles. This parallels the relation of the 
neck muscles to the Stomach Meridian, 
which is associated with emotions. The 
neck muscles are all involved in the sub-
tle changes in facial expressions involved 
in emotions. Pay attention to the facial 
expressions when testing the neck muscles 
and see what metaphors are generated. C2 Insertion s s Posterior 
Neck Extensors MUSCLE METAPHOR     
QUESTIONS TO CONSIDER C2 Observe your posture. Origin Are you literally sticking your neck out? Figuratively, are you taking too many risks, or do 
you need to take more chances?

5 Stomach Meridian Brachioradialis MUSCLE FUNCTION
Flexes the elbow joint and helps turn 
the wrist. Ability to turn the palm 
both forward and backward, depend-
ing on the start position. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Brachioradialis Brachioradialis T12 SITTING / STANDING / LYING FACE UP INDICATIONS 
A weakness of this muscle may make it 
difficult to get the arm up and behind 
the back. Weakness is frequently 
found in people who experience insom-
nia and general nervous tension. Wrist 
problems may arise. 1. Arm is bent to slightly more than 90˚, thumb toward shoulder. 2. Brace at the elbow. 3. Pressure is on the thumb side of the wrist to straighten the arm. The thumb side of the wrist. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P FOODS FOR BALANCING
Vitamin B-6 may be important if wrist 
problems are present with this weak-
ness. P P NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS o
o Frontal Eminences: 
Sometimes felt as a 
slight bulge of the 
sides of the forehead 
between the eyebrows 
and the natural 
hairline. Hold until you feel a pulse. May 
be repeated until emotional stress does not 
inhibit the intact muscle. Test Also:  Pectoralis Major Clavicular, Sartorius, and other muscles associated with adrenal function.

text:
Touch for Health 
Letters from Around the World! TFH is a Joy and a Way of Life When I first learned Touch for Health, I 
had already made a quantum jump away from 
an office-bound career in London. I had 
become aware of how unhappy I was with my 
life and for years had suffered severe back 
pain. I set about exploring alternatives to 
my life style and attitudes, studying many 
aspects of the then new human potential 
movement and eventually becoming a teacher of 
massage and healing. I continued studying when 
I moved to the US in the early 70’s. And then 
I met Touch for Health! TFH Transforms Hope to Trust I first discovered Touch for Health and 
Kinesiology in a little town in Holland. I 
witnessed something “magic.” The presenter 
checked people’s arms and legs, which were often 
weak, massaged certain points, and their muscles 
got stronger. I was so overwhelmed and so angry 
that it was so easy to balance people and I’d 
never known. I just had to participate in a 
course the same week to confirm that I truly 
had witnessed this. The class changed my hope 
that “all is possible” to trust. I have been 
balancing people ever since. It is my life, my 
joy and I brought TFH to Denmark. From the very first class I thought “This is 
it!”  At last here was a system of healing 
where the body itself could show what it 
needed. I helped my studies by going weekly 
for a treatment with my teacher. After about 
10 sessions I realized that for the first 
time in my adult life I was free from pain. In 1978 I became a TFH Instructor, returning 
to England in 1980 to teach and practice. Since then TFH has become a way of life. I 
have traveled the world with it, kept my body 
in good condition, and had the privilege of 
teaching hundreds of students. What a joy 
Touch for Health has been for me! Natalie Davenport
England Grethe Fremming 
Denmark

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H 
119
E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H Spleen Meridian The Spleen Meridian serves 
a very important func-
tion. While the stomach 
receives raw materials, 
the spleen functions to 
transform them into 
useable forms and to 
distribute them. It is a moment 
in the cycle of 
assimilating what 
is 
appropriate 
and identifying 
toxic or harmful 
elements to be 
eliminated. The 
Spleen Function 
also relates to 
immune 
func-
tions, 
purifying 
the blood, increas-
ing power of the 
white 
blood 
cells 
and removing the 
damaged and dead 
red 
blood 
cells. Pancreas 
function 
is also related to the 
Spleen 
Meridian 
and is involved in sugar metabolism, particularly, and 
digestion in general, breaking things 
down into manageable parts. ELEMENT METAPHOR The Earth Element metaphor corre-
sponds to the foundation, the ground, 
the soil, the dirt. The components that 
form the Earth (salts, minerals and 
ores), symbolize Metal which arises 
from the Earth Element. The Earth 
Element is controlled by Wood which 
is symbolized by the roots or trees 
holding the earth in position. The Earth 
Element in turn controls water like a 
container, giving it form, as in a lake or 
a river. The Earth element is associated 
with the Season of Late Summer, and is 
said to be a time of transition not only 
between Summer and Autumn, but also 
between each of the seasons. The direc-
tion associated with the Earth Element 
is neither the traditional North, South, 
East, or West, but Center. Thus, the 
five Elements are sometimes repre-
sented with the Earth Element at the 
center, with the other four Elements at 
the cardinal directions.

METAPHOR
5 Spleen Meridian PEAK FUNCTION: 9 -11AM 5-ELEMENT METAPHORS ENERGY FLOW:  YIN ELEMENT METAPHOR: EARTH E	 In your current phase of change, do you 
feel like you have enough roots to nourish 
your growth and enough stability to give 
form to your dreams? Color	
Yellow: What might the color yellow represent? Season	
Late Summer: Is it time for the harvest, or do you need to let things develop a 	
	
	
little longer? E	 Do you feel like you have your feet on the 
ground or do you need to be more grounded 
and centered? Climate	
Dampness/Humidity: Do you need more steam for your goals, or is fog 	
	
	
	
hampering your progress? E	 How are you doing with keeping your act 
cleaned up? Odor	
Fragrant:  Do you need to “smell the flowers” or focus on the effort to bring in 	
	
	
the harvest? RELATED MUSCLES Latissimus Dorsi Trapezius: Middle / Lower Taste	
Sweet: What tastes sweet in your life/goals? Opponens Pollicis Longus Emotion	
Sympathy/Empathy: Do you need to relate to others’ feelings or do you need 	
	
	
empathy/sympathy? Triceps QUESTIONS TO CONSIDER
SPLEEN MERIDIAN
METAPHORS Sound	
Singing: Do you need enthusiasm, expression, singing, or do you “sing” or tell 	
	
	
too much? E	 Do you have enough sweetness or too 
much sweetness? Fortifies	
Muscles: Do you need more power/movement or do you need stillness and 	 	
	
	
patience? E	 How are you at breaking down problems 
into digestible parts? Personal Power	
Decrease: What can you let go of to allow you to have more personal power in 	
	
	
your life? E	 Are you burdening yourself with toxic mate-
rials and overworking the detox/immune 
system function in dietary, mental, chemical 
or spiritual areas? Faith/Worldview	
Late Adolescence or Conventional/Synthetic Faith: What are your values vs. 	
	
	
the values of your peer group, community or culture?

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H
122 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 4 Spleen Meridian  Latissimus Dorsi MUSCLE FUNCTION
Holds the shoulder down and helps 
keep the back straight. It is involved 
in all the movements of the arm across 
the front of the body. It is used in 
all forceful arm movements such as 
swimming, rowing, bowling, golf and 
baseball swings, and chopping. When 
it is out of balance posture is effected 
from the shoulders to the pelvis. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Latissimus Dorsi 4 STANDING OR LYING FACE UP T7 1. Position the straight arm beside the body, elbow in, thumb back, 
palm out. 2. Be sure there is no tension in the shoulder girdle, or bending of elbow. 3. Stabilize on the same shoulder. 4. Pressure is at the forearm to pull the arm slightly  forward and 
away from the body. INDICATIONS 
Latissimus Dorsi is associated with 
Pancreas Function, sugar metabolism 
and digestion in general, including insu-
lin production. People with diabetes, 
hyperinsulinism (over-production of insu-
lin, hypoglycemia (low blood sugar) or 
other problems with sugar metabolism 
may show a weak Latissimus Dorsi. You 
may see a high shoulder on the weak 
side. This weakness is very common, 
and it can often be an indicator for aller-
gies as well as an intolerance for sugar, 
caffeine, or tobacco. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Inside of the forearm above the wrist. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Foods rich in vitamin A such as yellow 
and green leafy vegetables, tripe and 
sweetbreads. Avoid refined sugars or 
other concentrated sweets. P
P o NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS On the Parietal bone, 
above and just behind 
the ear. Test Also:  Opposite side Upper Trapezius, Triceps, Sartorius, Gracilis, Pectoralis Major Clavicular.
section: 3. Stabilize on the same shoulder.

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H
124 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Spleen Meridian Middle/Lower Trapezius MUSCLE FUNCTION
This is one of the muscles that keeps 
the shoulder blade in and turns it. It 
can be involved in shoulder and arm 
problems. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Middle Trapezius STANDING  / LYING FACE UP 1. With the arm straight out to the side and the thumb toward the 
head. 2. Pressure is against the back of the wrist to push it forwards while 
bracing the front of the shoulder on the  same side. INDICATIONS 
Because of its relationship to the 
spleen, the Trapezius is important to 
balance in infections, sore throats, ane-
mia, and fevers. Continued weak-
ness, which may cause tension in the 
Pectoralis Major, may be due to a 
problem in the back at T12 and L1, the 
level of the last ribs. Sometimes this 
can be released by walking and letting 
the arms swing freely, although chiro-
practic adjustment may be necessary. T5
T6 Lower Trapezius STANDING / LYING FACE UP T6 1. Place the arm straight out to the side, rotate palm up toward the head. 2. Pressure is at the wrist to press the arm towards the head. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Back of the wrist. MIDDLE
LOWER FOODS FOR BALANCING
With colds or infections, the juice of 
half a lemon in 8 oz. of water every 
2-3 hours, with no food for 24 hours, 
is often helpful. Foods rich in vitamin 
C (citrus and green pepper) and buck-
wheat should be eaten as a general 
rule. P P P P o P NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS 1/2” above the Posterior 
Fontanel, the back soft 
spot on the baby’s head. Test Also:  Serratus Anterior, Rhomboids and Pectoralis Major Clavicular.
section: 3. Stabilize on the same shoulder.

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H
126 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Spleen Meridian Opponens Pollicis Longus MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS
MUSCLE FUNCTION
Allows gripping with the thumb and 
opposing little finger. Opponens Pollicis Longus INDICATIONS 
This weakness sometimes leads to “ten-
nis elbow.” If muscles on both hands are 
weak and do not respond, it may indicate 
a fixation of the sacrum. Rolling back 
and forth on the floor with the knees 
drawn up to the chest may help this; 
if not, a chiropractor should be seen. This muscle is particularly associated 
with Pancreas function, having to do 
with sugar metabolism and digestion 
functions of breaking things down into 
manageable parts, and separating what 
is useful and what is not. STANDING / SITTING / LYING FACE UP C4 1. Hold the tip of the thumb to tip of little finger, making a ring. 2. Test by pulling thumb and little finger apart. 3. A small amount of separation on the test is normal; check to see if 
the muscles lock at the point of separation. If so, the muscle may 
be considered strong. The underside of the thumb & small 
finger. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P STANDING WITH WRIST FLEXED
SUPINE WITH WRIST NEUTRAL FOODS FOR BALANCING
Vitamin A, vitamin C, vitamin B6, cal-
cium, dolomite (calcium & magnesium). Raw bone supplements may be needed, 
as well as foods rich in calcium, such as 
milk products and bone meal. P P o NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS 1/2” above the 
Posterior Fontanel, the 
soft spot on the back 
of baby’s head. Test Also:  None
section: 2. Test by pulling thumb and little finger apart.

E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5
Spleen Meridian Opponens Pollicis Longus 9-11AM
YIN
A MUSCLE ORIGIN AND INSERTION SPINAL 
REFLEX
POINT NEUROLYMPHATIC

between the 7th and 
8th ribs near the rib 
margin. C4 Origin: Carpal (wrist) bones within the

finger and thumb together. T7-T8 MUSCLE METAPHOR

T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H
128 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5
Spleen Meridian Triceps MUSCLE FUNCTION
These muscles in the back of the arm 
help in straightening the elbow, working 
opposite the Biceps. The motion of the 
test suggests reaching out or drawing in. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Triceps SITTING / STANDING / LYING FACE UP T1 1. With the arm partly flexed, palm towards the face. INDICATIONS 
This muscle is particularly associated 
with Pancreas function, having to do 
with sugar metabolism and digestion in 
general. The digestion metaphor has to 
do with breaking things down into man-
ageable parts. 2. Brace behind the elbow. 3. Pressure is against the back of the wrist to further bend the 
elbow. ON CHILDREN, have the arm almost straight. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Against the back of the wrist. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Foods rich in vitamin A such as green 
pepper, green and yellow vegetables, and 
liver. Avoid sweets and concentrated or 
refined sugars. P P o NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS On the Parietal bone 
just above and behind 
the ear. Test Also:  Levator Scapulae, Latissimus Dorsi, and Rhomboids
section: 2. Brace behind the elbow.

text:
Touch for Health 
Letters from Around the World! Touch for Health is “Wunderbar!” When I read the Touch for Health book in 
1990, my life was in transition. I was not 
happy with my life as a banker and I was 
often ill. My private life was a disaster, 
every relationship broke and I never wanted 
to be a father. In 1986 I had attended a 
massage course and I realized that touching 
people in that way gave me a good feeling. But I knew I needed an additional method for 
my future job as a masseur. In 1991 I took 
my first Touch for Health class. During that 
period I decided to risk the step into self-
employment and opened my practice. Whenever 
I had a problem, someone gave me a 14-muscle 
balance. My practice grew and since 1995 
I’ve also been teaching Touch for Health as 
a TFH Instructor. In 1996 and 1998 I joined 
the 6-day program with Dr. John Thie. From 
that moment on I knew Touch for Health is 
“wunderbar.” TFH Helps Me 
Be a Truly Holistic Practitioner I began to study medicine to become a holistic 
practitioner, to help people heal on all levels 
and find a healthy and happy lifestyle. I 
studied many valuable naturopathic systems but 
something was missing. When I discovered TFH it 
was a revelation. Something I could use to help 
people, but that anybody could do at home if 
they wish. TFH concretely showed the connection 
between body and mind, and could easily bring 
people down to earth from psychological problems 
and theories, giving them back their power and 
happiness beyond expectation. The more you 
practice the more you find TFH applications 
for every problem and person. And the most 
meaningful conversations spring up after one or 
two questions concerning the meridian energies 
from TFH, and my clients feel respected as real 
people. This was the realization of my dream. I use Touch for Health daily in my practice 
and I’m continually amazed when people feel 
better after a 14 or 42 muscle balance. Since 
1992 I work once a year with the world’s best 
Badminton players at the Badminton Swiss Open 
in Basel/Switzerland. Some of the players 
say Touch for Health is simply “magic”. I 
am a member of the Swiss Association of 
Kinesiologist SBVK and I created the Back-
Balance with the Touch for Health-techniques. I am grateful to Mrs. Carrie and Dr. John 
F. Thie for the wonderful work they started 
years ago. Thank you. Now I give classes in communication, family and 
organization constellation, meditation and TFH—
and the principles of TFH are the golden thread 
which holds it all together. Thomas Bissinger, M.D. Germany Thomas Bissinger, M.D. Germany Ueli Meier-Estrada
Founding Member and President
Swiss Association of Non-Medical Kinesiology
section: 2. Brace behind the elbow.

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H 
131
E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H Heart Meridian The Heart Meridian  involves 
the cycling of the blood as well as elec-
trical communication with the rest of 
the cells of the whole Soul. The heart 
is in constant communication with all of the organs and muscles to determine 
their blood/oxygen needs from moment 
to moment. The heart generates 5000 
times more powerful electrical messages 
throughout the Soul than the brain. In 
the Chinese belief system, the heart is 
also strongly associated with mental 
processes, while in the West we have 
the traditional metaphor of the heart 
as an emotional center and a center of 
wisdom. In Chinese medicine, the heart 
is called the “sovereign ruler” which 
directs action with clear insight. ELEMENT METAPHOR The Fire Element can be represented 
by the image of the hot, red sun, giving 
both light and warmth. It is associated 
with all of life’s heat and passions, par-
ticularly with enthusiasm, vitality and 
“warm feelings.” The Wood Element 
provides the fuel for Fire and the Earth 
Element is formed from the ashes gener-
ated by Fire. The Metal Element is con-
trolled by the intense heat of Fire which 
melts, purifies, and transforms Metal by 
forging it into a new and sturdy shape. Water controls Fire by tempering or 
quenching it.
section: 2. Brace behind the elbow.

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H
134 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 5 Heart Meridian Subscapularis MUSCLE FUNCTION
Allows the shoulder blade to glide over the 
rib cage and turn the arm, drawing the arm 
in when it is raised above the shoulder. It is 
hidden under the shoulder blade and cannot 
be directly observed or touched. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Subscapularis 5 SITTING / STANDING / LYING FACE UP T2 1. Arm out to the side, with elbow bent to 90˚ and as level as 
	
comfortable with shoulder neutral, hand toward the feet. INDICATIONS 
This muscle is particularly associated with 
Heart Meridian Function, which involves 
the circulation of the blood and electrical 
communication throughout the whole Soul, 
as well as the traditional metaphor of the 
heart as an emotional center. Palpitations, 
dizziness, shoulder or chest pains, and bleed-
ing gums are associated with Subscapularis 
imbalance. The heart is not only responsible 
for the circulating blood, but also affects the 
fluid flow of the acupuncture meridians. The 
heart is a muscular organ and it functions 
both from an external nerve supply like the 
other muscles, and also contains an inde-
pendent nerve system regulating itself. 2. Brace the elbow to stabilize the arm. 3. Pressure is at the forearm to rotate the forearm forwards and up. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Under forearm the inside of the wrist. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Foods which contain calcium, vitamins E 
and B from brewer’s yeast, wheat germ, 
bone, dairy products, egg yolk, green leafy 
vegetables, peas, beans, nuts, strawberries, 
lemons and oranges. Buckweat and citrus 
are good, particularly with bleeding gums. P P o NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS Anterior Fontanel, the 
“baby’s soft spot” on 
the top of the head. Test Also:  Abdominals, Quadriceps, Supraspinatus, and Psoas.
section: 3. Pressure is at the forearm to rotate the forearm forwards and up.

text:
Touch for Health 
Letters from Around the World! All of My Injuries Vanished with TFH! I was born in a schoolhouse as a child of 
two teachers – my mother was my teacher and 
my father the principal – I “had to become” 
a teacher and struggled later on to become 
principal too. I started a political career 
as an elected representative of my community. During my military career I became the Cultural 
Protection Officer and then the Military 
Geographer of Vienna, reaching the rank of 
captain. As you can imagine, I had a lot of 
stress, and it began to make me ill. I developed 
troubles with my heart, high blood pressure, my 
liver and an injured nerve in my leg. More dead than alive, my wife took me to a 
kinesiologist, who massaged the Neurolymphatics 
of the peroneus so hard, that I nearly climbed up 
the walls. She told me her admired, charismatic 
teacher Dr. John F. Thie had taught her to do it 
that way and within a moment I felt a change. The lame me was healed. Using TfH and kinesiology 
methods, all my injuries vanished. That is why I 
became a dynamic supporter of Touch for Health 
and began practicing TfH seriously. I became a 
Touch for Health Instructor Trainer and founded 
with Mrs. Bichler–Lajda the Austrian Academy of 
Kinesiology and Health with it’s two aims: 1, 
teaching and promoting TfH. and Kinesiology to as 
many people as possible; and 2, helping people 
to activate their self–healing power inside 
themselves by applying Touch for Health and other 
kinesiology methods. The way I’m living and working now involves 
stress too, but it is stress that 
satisfies me and makes me happy and healthy. Ortwin F. Niederhuber
Vienna, Austria A Headache? TFH. Pain? TFH. A Disease? TFH. We are not against allopathic medicine and 
drugs but we prefer to use it only if it is 
really necessary. And since we are using TFH, 
we do not need drugs anymore. Of course if a 
day comes when we need allopathic medicine, 
we will use it, and it will be much more 
effective than on somebody that is using 
drugs all the time. From time to time, my 
daughter Eva (7) comes back from school sad 
saying, “Dad I need a TFH balance!”  Five 
minutes later (the time to balance her) the 
happiness comes back. Noa (2) is used to 
being balanced. Last month she was very sick 
at night. At two in the morning, we balanced 
her and immediately, as I was balancing the 
last muscle, her sickness subsided and she 
fell asleep. I am teaching TFH to mothers 
as a family method that helps people to be 
balanced and in good health.
section: 3. Pressure is at the forearm to rotate the forearm forwards and up.

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H 
137
E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Small Intestine Meridian The Small Intestine Meridian 
is primarily related to absorption of 
nutritious substances and the separation 
of waste material. These same functions 
take place on the cellular level, and in the Soul as a whole. It is the assimila-
tion of influences and stuff from outside 
the Soul. The small intestine winds for 
over 23 feet and has three main parts, 
the Duodenum, the Jejunum, and the 
Ileum. Throughout the small intestine, 
various secretions from other organs are 
added to the liquefied foods, which aid 
in the absorption of nutrients. Bacteria 
make up a large amount of the contents 
of the small intestine, also acting upon 
the materials that enter so that they can 
be absorbed. ELEMENT METAPHOR The Fire Element can be represented 
by the image of the hot, red sun, giving 
both light and warmth. It is associated 
with all of life’s heat and passions, par-
ticularly with enthusiasm, vitality and 
“warm feelings.” The Wood Element 
provides the fuel for Fire and the Earth 
Element is formed from the ashes gener-
ated by Fire. The Metal Element is con-
trolled by the intense heat of Fire which 
melts, purifies, and transforms Metal by 
forging it into a new and sturdy shape. Water controls Fire by tempering or 
quenching it.
section: 3. Pressure is at the forearm to rotate the forearm forwards and up.

142 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Small Intestine Meridian Abdominals MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS
MUSCLE FUNCTION
The Abdominal Muscles provide sup-
port for the organs, keeping the pelvis 
up and compressing the Abdomen. The 
Rectus Abdominis spans up and down 
the torso; the Transverse Abdominis are 
underneath going crosswise. Rectus Abdominis T6 STANDING 1. With hands on opposite shoulders, lean forward ensuring neck 
muscles do not flex. 2. Stabilize by bracing the lower back. INDICATIONS
They are associated with the duode-
num, the first third of the small intestine 
just beyond the stomach in the diges-
tive tract. They are commonly involved 
in indigestion, “stomach” aches, and 
breathing difficulties. Weakness of these 
muscles can result in a feeling of limita-
tion or pain in the low back. If weakness 
is only on one side, there may be some 
restriction of shoulder movement on the 
opposite side. On women, this area can 
become flabby during pregnancy, losing 
its tone and making delivery more diffi-
cult. A man may develop a “beer belly.” 3. Pressure is against the arms at the midline while pushing straight 
back. LYING FACE UP 1. With knees bent, heels towards buttocks, arms crossed to 
opposite shoulders and leaning back, chin up. 2. Stabilize by bracing firmly on the lower thigh. 3. Pressure is against the arms at the midline while pushing straight 
back. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Against the arms at the midline. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Vitamin E as found in brewer’s yeast, 
wheat germ, yogurt, buttermilk, liver. P P NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS
Parietal Eminence, 
usually a prominent 
ridge between the 
ear and the top of the 
head. o Test Also:  Sacrospinalis, Hamstrings; check Popliteus with knee pain.

T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H
144 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Small Intestine Meridian Abdominals Help for Those Nearly half the population has an additional abdominal muscle that assists in keeping the 
lower Abdomen flat. It is a very small pyramid shaped muscle called the Pyramidalis that 
is located below the navel. This little-known muscle plays a key role in helping people 
maintain a healthy posture and a strong lower back but is still a mystery as to why not 
everyone has one. with Protruding  
Lower Abdominal 
Areas Testing is done only in the seated position on a flat surface with the legs spread apart as 
far as possible and the arms crossed on the chest as in the testing of the Rectus portion 
of the Abdominals. Lean as far forward as possible between your legs, as if to lay your 
forehead flat on the floor. Note how far forward you can bend. Pressure is against the 
crossed arms to straighten the torso, stabilizing at the low back. While holding the posi-
tion, measure how close the head is to the surface and make a note of it. After applying 
the special reflex for the Abdominals, measure and test again, noting the difference. If 
retesting using this method shows weakness, other reflexes for the abdominal muscles 
are used until no inhibition is shown after 10 repeated tests. This procedure is very helpful 
in increasing flexibility and helping to achieve a flat abdominal area below the navel. Women who have experienced full term pregnancies often benefit greatly from these 
exercises. When abdominal fat is lost through dieting, this protocol is a very important 
technique for improving better posture and a stronger low back.

text:
Touch for Health 
Letters from Around the World! Help for Injury 
and a New Direction in Life Just as I prepared to enter college on an 
athletic scholarship, I injured my lower 
abdomen while exercising. Doctors told me 
there was nothing they could do and that 
I would have to live with the pain and 
diminished flexibility. Touch for Health is Natural Healing In the early 1980’s, I injured my back 
while working as an organ builder. After a 
lengthy period of pain and suffering, I went 
to a natural healer. After two sessions which 
seemed very strange and odd to me, I was 
completely back in shape. Then I ran across the 
Touch for Health manual. My enthusiasm increased 
all the more when TFH was recommended to me 
during my training as a yoga instructor with 
Yogi Bhajan! So, I decided to take formal TFH courses. There 
I met my wife, Karin. She was my TFH instructor. Today Karin and I have a joint naturopathic 
practice in which we use TFH with wonderful 
results. Then, in 1976, my wife and I were on an 
adventure touring the National Parks of the 
western U.S. and we met a man who practiced 
Touch for Health. He briefly explained the 
concepts, tested four muscles and fixed my 
injury in only a few minutes! We immediately bought Dr. Thie’s book and have 
worn out three copies over the years! TFH has 
been a constant companion and even when we go 
on a trip we make sure that we take along the 
TFH book. Earl and Gail 
www.etouchforhealth.com Georg Weitzsch 
www.integrative.de Lena Jorgensen

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H 
147
E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H Bladder Meridian The Bladder Meridian func-
tion is to store waste liquid before elimi-
nation, not only in the urinary bladder but 
also in all of the cells and throughout the 
Soul. In the Chinese system, the Bladder 
is said to be the storehouse of emotions, 
and so the Bladder Function is involved 
in the water balance/emotional balance in 
the whole Soul. Water is eliminated as it 
reaches excessive volume, or when waste 
material is highly concentrated. The urinary bladder is always “full” since 
the muscles expand or contract as more 
liquid is received from the kidneys. By 
drinking more pure water, the bladder is 
actually strengthened through exercise of 
the expanding bladder muscles. Water is 
a potent symbol for emotion and mystery 
in life. When we are able to accept more 
of the vast mystery of existence, and allow 
our emotions to flow, we find our toler-
ance and flexibility increase. ELEMENT METAPHOR The Water Element is symbolized by the 
ocean, sea, lakes, and rivers. The Metal 
Element is said to create water, which 
we might visualize as water condensing 
on a cold metal surface, or bubbling 
up from the same depths in the earth 
where ores and salts are condensed. Water is controlled and formed by the 
Earth much like a container. Water 
controls fire by quenching it. Because 
water has a variety of properties, it is 
very mysterious and can be a symbol of 
the unconscious, of our dream-life, of 
our emotions, of that which we do not 
understand and that which we fear, yet 
it remains an essential element to our 
daily living.

150 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 7 Bladder Meridian	 Peroneus MUSCLE FUNCTION
The Peroneus (aka Fibularis) flexes the 
side of the foot upward and out and is 
associated with the maintaining of foot 
and ankle balance. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Peroneus 7 T12 SITTING / STANDING / LYING FACE UP STANDING 1. Begin with toes turned to the side, little toe flexed toward the head. INDICATIONS 
Weakness will cause the foot to turn in, 
especially in children, and can also be 
associated with foot and ankle problems, 
which can result in an upward misuse and 
misalignment of the entire posture. 2. Ensure the big toe is not pulled up. 3. Stabilize the foot by holding the heel in one hand, or rest heel
	
against the floor and brace inside of heel. 4. Pressure is against the outside top of the foot just behind the toes,
	
pressing down and in toward the midline. LYING FACE UP FOODS FOR BALANCING
Foods rich in thiamine (peas, beans, 
wheat germ, whole grains, raw fruit, 
cabbage, egg yolk, yeast), calcium (bone, 
dairy products, egg yolk, green leafy veg-
etables, beans, nuts). Avoid foods con-
taining oxalic acid (cranberries, coffee, 
chocolate, purple fruits) at meals when 
calcium is consumed. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Outside top of foot, above base of toes. P NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS P The Frontal Eminence, 
usually a bulge between 
the eyebrows and 
the hairline, and the 
Glabella, a flat area 
closer to the center and 
just above the eyebrows. o
o
o
o Test Also:  Piriformis and Sacrospinalis

T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H
152 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Bladder	 Meridian Sacrospinalis MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS
MUSCLE FUNCTION
The Sacrospinalis is actually a group of sev-
eral separate muscles along the backbone. When balanced, you can lie on your belly 
and raise your entire chest off the ground in 
a posterior arch. This action helps the spinal 
discs get proper nourishment. These many 
small muscles work together to help keep 
the whole back erect. Sacrospinalis T12 STANDING 1. With feet shoulder width apart, hands in small of back, keep hips 
square and twist torso back and down with head tilted back to 
same side. 2. Push the shoulder foreward while bracing the opposite hip. 3. The person may be braced against a wall, pressing the shoulder 
toward the wall. INDICATIONS 
Weakness in these muscles is associated 
with 19 different areas of pain. Malfunction 
in the spine is associated with arthritis, rheu-
matism, bursitis, shoulder and elbow prob-
lems, and even snapping fingers. Weakness 
on one side will cause a sideways bending 
of the spine that can become a serious prob-
lem if neglected and will create the posture 
seen on many skinny people, the head and 
hips forward with the spine pushed back. The Sacrospinalis is closely associated with 
bladder problems but may also be affected 
by emotional strain. LYING FACE DOWN 1. Both hands placed in the small of the back. 2. Lift one shoulder, and look back over it. 3. Pressure is against the back of the shoulder, pressing it toward 
the table or floor while stabilizing the opposite hip. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Back of the shoulder. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Foods rich in vitamins A and C such as 
green leafy vegetables, citrus fruits, and 
green peppers. P P NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS o
o The Frontal Eminence, 
usually a bulge between 
the eyebrows and the 
hairline. Test Also:  Abdominals, Quadraceps, Hamstrings, Anterior and Posterior Tibials, Peroneus, Gluteus Maximus.

154 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Bladder Meridian Tibials MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS
MUSCLE FUNCTION
Anterior Tibial
The Anterior Tibial flexes the foot out 
and upward and is often found weak on 
both sides. Anterior Tibial L5 STANDING STANDING / LYING FACE UP 1. With the leg straight and the toes and ankle flexed toward the knee. Posterior Tibial
The Posterior Tibial acts as a stabilizer of 
the ankle, helps point the toe, and turns 
the foot inward. 2. Stabilize at the heel. 3. Pressure is against the top of the foot to push the toes downward. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Against top of foot. INDICATIONS 
Weakness may be associated with rectal 
fissures and urethra and bladder prob-
lems. Bunions may be present, especially 
if weakness is causing a “flat foot” or 
fallen arches. The Posterior Tibial may be 
involved in pronation of the foot. It is 
often found over-tight rather than weak. LYING FACE UP P P FOODS FOR BALANCING
Vitamin E as found in green peas, green 
leafy vegetables, and wheat germ. Posterior Tibial L5 STANDING / LYING FACE UP STANDING 1. With foot turned in and down as far as possible, stabilize at the heel. 2. Pressure is up and out on the ball of the foot just behind the big toe. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Against ball and inside of foot. P LYING FACE UP o
o NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS P Frontal Eminence, 
between the  eyebrows 
and the hairline. Test Also:  Sacropinalis, Peroneus, Psoas

text:
Touch for Health 
Letters from Around the World! Touch for Health Gives Me a 
Structure to Understand the Energy 		
I Can See TFH is My Primary Tool Two peculiarities from my history prepared 
me for my first Touch for Health class in 
1977. First, I have always been able to see 
energy. I know a person much more from their 
energy than from their personality or even 
their behavior. Secondly, I have had multiple 
health challenges all my life which Western 
medicine had not been able to help. I’d had headaches for years and couldn’t 
get help anywhere. In 1978, I found relief 
through reflexology. I was studying to be a 
medical laboratory technician and my desire was 
to become a medical doctor, to build bridges 
between the medical world and the complementary 
world. In 1982, I began studying to become a 
reflexologist, while working in a lab. Grethe 
Fremming came to give a talk and demonstration 
about TFH to the reflexology students. Touch for Health gave me a structure to 
understand what I have always seen. I learned 
that the highways of energy I see surging 
through the body are called meridians in 
Chinese medicine. And while I did not need 
muscle testing to determine if the energies 
of a meridian were disturbed, it gave me a 
way to demonstrate to others the health of 
their meridians. Most importantly, I not only 
learned how to understand what I saw, but also 
how to make corrections when required. My eyes and mouth fell wide open while Grethe 
tested different muscles and rubbed or held 
different points. Each time, the muscles 
wouldn’t hold, yet after the rubbing, the 
muscles could hold. I had to try this magic. After a few sessions with Grethe, I had no hay 
fever in the springtime. Then I had to learn 
TFH! I quit the lab and started to work with 
reflexology and Kinesiology - primarily using 
TFH. In November of 1986, I became a TFH 
instructor. Over the years, I have gathered 
thousands of magical stories. For example, a 
woman came to me with deep fissures in her hands 
because of her cleaning work. After two sessions 
with just TFH, the fissures were gone. This was 
magic to her and me. It still is! I have now taught Energy Medicine around the 
world to tens of thousands of people and my 
book on the topic has been translated into 
many languages. But my gratitude to Touch 
for Health is enormous, not only because it 
was the first system I learned, but because 
I still know of no other system that more 
beautifully introduces people to energy 
techniques for caring for themselves. Thank you, John, for teaching me to keep it 
simple.

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H 
157
E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H Kidney Meridian The Kidney Meridian is 
involved in controlling the volume, com-
position, and pressure of fluids in all 
the cells as well as the whole Soul and 
is important in growth, development 
and reproductive functions. Blood flows through the kidneys at its highest pres-
sure, toxins are filtered out, and nour-
ishing materials are directed to where 
they are needed. Water is symbolic of 
mystery, emotion and spirit. Kidney 
Meridian Function is involved in the bal-
ance of these aspects of life. In Chinese 
medicine the Kidney is also said to be a 
storehouse of life force and has a strong 
spiritual aspect. ELEMENT METAPHOR The Water Element is symbolized by the 
ocean, sea, lakes, and rivers. The Metal 
Element is said to create water, which 
we might visualize as water condensing 
on a cold metal surface, or bubbling 
up from the same depths in the earth 
where ores and salts are condensed. Water is controlled and formed by the 
Earth much like a container. Water 
controls fire by quenching it. Because 
water has a variety of properties, it is 
very mysterious and can be a symbol of 
the unconscious, of our dream-life, of 
our emotions, of that which we do not 
understand and that which we fear, yet 
it remains an essential element to our 
daily living.

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H
160 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 8 Kidney Meridian Psoas MUSCLE FUNCTION
The Psoas helps flex the hips and keeps the 
lumbar curve in the spine. This muscle both 
flexes the thigh on the trunk, and the trunk 
on the thigh, thus it has paradoxical origins 
and insertions depending on the action (sit-
ting up, kicking to the side, or sidestepping 
motions). MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Psoas 8 T12 STANDING 1. Lift straight leg 45˚ forward and to the side, rotating outwards. Brace on the opposite hip. A chair for support may be helpful. 2. Pressure is against the inside of the leg just above the ankle to push 
the leg back and outwards. INDICATIONS 
With weakness on both sides, there will be 
a tendency for the lower back to flatten. Weakness on one side will cause the foot 
to turn in or make the hip low causing a 
strain on the Psoas. If not corrected, nag-
ging low back pains, kidney disturbances, 
inability to rest, and foot problems may 
arise. The kidneys are the blood filtration 
system. Improper functioning can also result 
in skin conditions like acne, pimples, boils, 
or eczema, and can aggravate hearing 
conditions. LYING FACE UP 1. Raise straight leg 45˚ to the side, and rotate outwards. Brace on 
the opposite hip. 2. Pressure is against the inside of leg just above the ankle to gently 
push the leg down and out. Catch and support the leg if there is 
pain or it begins to fall. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Outward against the inside of the          
leg near the ankle. CAUTON: 
The Quadriceps muscle 
is a synergistic muscle 
and if it is painful or felt 
strongly contracting in 
the test position use 
caution and consider 
this an indicator of an 
inhibition of the Psoas. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Water (preferably purified) in ample quanti-
ties on a regular basis, kidney meat, foods 
containing vitamin A and E such as parsley, 
green peppers, green leafy vegetables, wheat 
germ and green peas. Avoid coffee and other 
strong beverages. P P NEUROVASCULAR  
HOLDING POINTS o Occipital Protuberance, 
the prominent bump on 
the back of the head 
near the base of 
the skull. Test Also:  Fascia Lata and Upper Trapezius

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H
162 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Kidney Meridian Upper Trapezius MUSCLE FUNCTION
The Upper Trapezius tilts the chin and 
pulls in the shoulder blade. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Upper Trapezius INDICATIONS 
Upper Trapezius is associated with symp-
toms related to the eyes and ears – eye 
strain, conjunctivitis, some blurred vision, 
ear infections, and some hearing prob-
lems, especially loss in the low 500 to 
2,000 cycle range. The inhibition may be 
found with repeated muscle testing. C7 SITTING / STANDING / LYING FACE UP 1. Shoulder brought up to the ear, then the head is tilted to the side 
(not turned). 2. Pressure is against the shoulder and the side of the head to try 
to pull them apart. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Against the shoulder and the side of         
the head. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Foods rich in vitamin A and B and also 
unsaturated fats (green leafy vegetables, 
eggs, whole grains, and liver) are helpful. People who also have trouble getting to 
sleep at night may be lacking in calcium 
(bone, dairy products, egg yolk, green

5 Kidney Meridian Iliacus MUSCLE FUNCTION
This muscle initiates the kicking motion and 
flexes the trunk towards the thighs. It is a 
small muscle yet may affect a wide range of 
movements. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Iliacus STANDING / LYING FACE DOWN T11 INDICATIONS 
This muscle is also associated with the function 
of the transfer of nutrients and wastes from the 
small intestine to large intestine at the cecum. When these functions are not normal it can 
result in seemingly bizarre symptoms almost 
anywhere, including nausea, sudden low back 
pain, shoulder pain, headache, sudden thirst, 
dark circles under the eyes and pallor. When 
vague symptoms are present this muscle may 
be an indicator of this energy imbalance in 
the subtle energy functions of the Kidney 
Meridian and its relationship to the Large 
Intestine Meridian in all of the cells. This may 
be thought of as subtle energy imbalances in 
the process of eliminating waste material. 1. Bend knee 90º, and turn lower leg as far out to the side as 
possible. If standing, a chair for support may be helpful. 2. Pressure is above the ankle to bring leg toward the midline, 
while stabilizing the inside of knee. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Against the outside of the ankle. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Oil or water-soluble chlorophyll as a supple-
ment may aid in restoring normal function, 
along with sour milk products such as kefir, 
yogurt and whey. P Avoid: Raw foods when this muscle is 
weak, especially raw vegetables and fruits 
high in enzymes such as papaya. P o NEUROVASCULAR  
HOLDING POINTS Parietal Eminence, 
usually a ridge between 
the ear and the top 
of the head. Test Also:  None

text:
Touch for Health 
Letters from Around the World! TFH Allows me to Help Myself, 
Friends, Clients and Students TFH Gives Me Confidence, 
Energy, Gratitude and Faith During my studies in the faculty of medicine 
in Geneva, Switzerland, I heard about a 
biofeedback Institute in San Francisco. I 
went there in 1978 and during the 3 months 
of studies, I discovered Touch For Health and 
Doctor John Thie. I decided to be part of 
this faculty and to practice and teach Touch 
for Health in my country. It was not easy to 
be a pioneer in Europe with this new vision 
of health. But today TFH is one of the most 
well-known healthcare methods in our country! When I first encountered Kinesiology, I was 
amazed at how simple health solutions turned 
out to be. I began to visit chiropractors who 
relieved me of everything from allergies to 
sport injuries to nightmares to illnesses. I took the basic 4 TFH levels of training from 
my local instructors, who happened to be John 
and Matthew Thie. I’ve gained wisdom, a career, 
respect for natural solutions, friends and 
loved ones to grow with, as well as confidence, 
energy, gratitude and faith. Jean-François Jaccard
Geneve Switzerland April Davis
Los Angeles, California

T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H 
167
E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H Circulation-Sex Meridian The Circulation-Sex Meridian 
pertains to the many functions that are 
intimately associated with the hormones 
and chemical messengers related to all 
reproduction whether it be appropriate 
balance in cell production or sexual pro-
creation. It has to do with the nourish- ment of new cells and the preparation for 
cell reproduction. It involves the menstru-
ation cycles, ovarian and uterine functions 
and prostate and testicular functions. This includes the pleasures of sexual love. It also has to do with passing on genetic, 
cultural, and personal heritage. The 
Circulation-Sex Meridian is sometimes 
also called the Pericardium Meridian and 
relates to the muscular function of the 
heart and blood vessels, that which keeps 
the beat steady and appropriate for the 
external and internal environments. ELEMENT METAPHOR The Fire Element can be represented 
by the image of the hot, red sun, giving 
both light and warmth. It is associated 
with all of life’s heat and passions, par-
ticularly with enthusiasm, vitality and 
“warm feelings.” The Wood Element 
provides the fuel for Fire and the Earth 
Element is formed from the ashes gener-
ated by Fire. The Metal Element is con-
trolled by the intense heat of Fire which 
melts, purifies, and transforms Metal by 
forging it into a new and sturdy shape. Water controls Fire by tempering or 
quenching it.

lation of blood, warmth, nutrition or sexual 
energy?

5 9 Circulation-Sex Meridian Gluteus Medius MUSCLE FUNCTION
Pulls the thigh out and rotates the leg. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Gluteus Medius INDICATIONS 
If there is weakness in this muscle, the 
hip and shoulder may be high, and there 
is also a tendency toward bowed legs 
or a peculiar limp. Menstrual cramps, 
prostate trouble, and impotency may be 
associated. There may be breast soreness, 
although this is more often associated 
with imbalance in Fascia Lata. 9 L5 STANDING 1. Position straight leg out to the side with no rotation. Brace on the 
opposite hip. A chair for support may be helpful. 2. Pressure is on the outside of the leg to bring it towards the midline. LYING FACE UP 1. Position straight leg out to the side with no rotation. Brace the 
opposite leg. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Foods rich in vitamin E (wheat germ, 
green peas, green leafy vegetables). For 
men, eating calf fry (Rocky Mountain 
Oysters) may also be helpful. 2. The weight of the leg should be felt in the tester’s hand. 3. Pressure is on the leg above the ankle to bring it towards the 
midline. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Against the outside lower leg. P P NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS o Parietal Eminence, 
usually a ridge between 
the ear and the top of 
the head. The widest 
part of the head. Test Also:  Psoas and Fascia Lata

172 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Circulation-Sex Meridian Adductors MUSCLE FUNCTION
The Adductors hold the thigh in, flexing 
it and rotating it inward. The muscle test 
involves holding the legs together. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Adductors L1 STANDING / LYING FACE UP INDICATIONS 
Weakness can make the pelvis tilt down, 
will sometimes complicate stiff shoulders 
and can even cause elbow pain. These 
muscles are essential to horseback riders. The continued strain of trying to stay in 
the saddle can result in weakness of the 
Adductors, causing the bowlegged posture 
so characteristic of cowboys. Problems 
with the reproductive organs, especially 
changes in hormone function or meno-
pause can affect the Adductors. 1. Hold the feet together and stabilize opposite leg. When 
standing, a chair for support may be helpful. 2. At the ankle, separate the mobile leg from the stabilized leg. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Against the inside of leg, above ankle to 
pull the leg away to the side. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Foods rich in vitamin E (wheat germ, 
green peas, green leafy vegetables) should 
be emphasized. For men, eating calf fry 
(Rocky Mountain Oysters) may also be 
helpful. P NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS P o
o Parietal Eminence, 
usually a ridge 
between the ear and 
the top of the head, 
and the Lambdoidal 
Suture on the sides at 
the back of the skull. Test Also:  The opposing muscles, Tensor Fascia Lata and Hamstrings
section: 2. At the ankle, separate the mobile leg from the stabilized leg.

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H
174 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Circulation-Sex Meridian Piriformis MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS
MUSCLE FUNCTION
This hip muscle is very important in posture, 
especially the position of the Sacrum. It 
is the uppermost of the hip rotators. In a 
seated position, it allows the leg to move 
outward. Piriformis S1 STANDING 1. Keeping the knees next to each other, create a 90º angle at the 
knee and fully rotate the knee to bring the lower leg inward. When 
standing, a chair for support may be helpful. INDICATIONS 
Weakness on one side can cause the sacrum 
to twist, making the ankle on that side turn 
in, the knees “knock” and the opposite foot 
turn out. The Piriformis is located right next to 
the sciatic nerve which is the longest, largest 
nerve in the body. In 10% of the population, 
the sciatic nerve passes through the belly of 
Piriformis. With a problem in the Piriformis, 
the sciatic nerve is often affected. There may 
be pain down the leg, numbness and tingling 
of the legs, burning urination, and other blad-
der problems. When seated, if you find it hard 
to lift a foot onto the opposite knee, it is a 
good indication of Piriformis inhibition. 2. Stabilize the outside of the knee. 3. Pressure is against the inside of the lower leg to rotate the leg out 
to the side. LYING FACE UP 1. With knee and hip bent at 90º and rotating knee in to bring the foot 
across the opposite leg as far as comfortable. 2. Heel should be higher than the knee. Stabilize the outside of the knee. 3. Pressure is against the inside of the lower leg to rotate it out to the 
side. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Against the inside of the lower leg. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Adding iodine supplement and/or foods rich 
in vitamin E (wheat germ, green peas, green 
leafy vegetables) should be emphasized. For men, eating calf fry (Rocky Mountain 
Oysters) may also be helpful. P P o
NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS Parietal Eminence, 
usually a ridge between 
the ear and the top of 
the head. NOTE:  If the Piriformis is tight or painful, check Hamstrings, Gluteus Medius and Adductors, espe-
cially if the person has difficulty placing their ankle on the opposite knee when sitting.
section: 2. Heel should be higher than the knee. Stabilize the outside of the knee.

5 Circulation-Sex Meridian Gluteus Maximus MUSCLE FUNCTION
The Gluteus Maximus the object of much 
joking is actually one of the largest and 
strongest muscles in the body. It stabilizes 
the lower back, extends the thigh, and 
helps pull the leg in. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Gluteus Maximus C2 STANDING / LYING FACE DOWN 1. Bend the leg 90˚, extending the thigh back as far as possible. When standing, a chair for support may be helpful. INDICATIONS 
Weakness on one side will twist the pelvis 
or make the crease of the buttocks go off to 
one side. With both sides weak, walking is 
difficult. This is sometimes due to stiffness 
of the neck bones, which may be helped 
by gently rotating the head and neck area, 
although chiropractic adjustment may be 
necessary. Problems with the sex organs, 
sex urges and drives, and prostate trouble 
may be related to this muscle. 2. Stabilize at the front of the hip when standing, OPPOSITE HIP 
WHEN LYING FACE DOWN. 3. Pressure is at the back of the thigh to bring it forward. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Against the back of the thigh. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Foods rich in vitamin E (wheat germ, green 
peas, green leafy vegetables) should be 
emphasized. For men, eating calf fry (Rocky 
Mountain Oysters) may also be helpful. P
P o NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS Lamboidal Suture, on 
the sides at the back of 
the skull. Test Also:  Adductors, Piriformis, and Neck Muscles

text:
Touch for Health 
Letters from Around the World! In Celebration 
of Touch for Health
	
	
When I took my first Touch for Health Class 
in 1979, I did not imagine that it would 
change my life and motivate me to a new 
career. I had studied at a local university 
and had planned on teaching. It did not work 
out that way! After taking the Touch for 
Health basic 14-hour self-help course, I went 
on to become a Touch for Health Instructor. I moved back to my homeland of Sweden and 
studied naturopathic medicine and became a 
naturopathic doctor. During my time of studies 
I taught many Touch for Health courses. Touch for Health 
Changed My Point of View I believe in Touch for Health Kinesiology 
because it changed my point of view about my 
life. I had serious problems with my back. The doctors said that I could not continue 
anymore with triathlons and could end up in a 
wheelchair. In 1997, I met a Kinesiologist in 
Mexico who gave me information about the Touch 
for Health system. A year later I went to Kiel, 
Germany, to participate in an International 
World University Triathlon championship. After 
that competition, I went to Basel/Switzerland 
and took my first Touch for Health class. I got 
some goal-balances about my situation. Today I 
feel good; the frequent back pain is gone. After working with patients now in excess of 
twenty years, I am still amazed, excited and 
thankful for Kinesiology techniques. I use 
Touch for Health muscle tests in my everyday 
clinic workups, along with the lab tests, dark 
field microscope work and other assessment 
tools. I still balance every patient before 
they leave the office. I met Mrs. Carrie and Dr. John Thie in 
Switzerland. When Dr. John gave me a balancing, 
I realized the most important thing for my life: 
if I believe in my goals and walk my way with 
confidence, it changes everything in my life. Sincerely, Peter Wilhelmsson, N.D. Falun Sweden Verónica Barba
Physio-Therapist
Guadalajara, México

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H 
179
E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Triple Warmer Meridian The Triple Warmer Meridian 
takes the form of three “heats,” the heat 
of metabolism, maintaining body heat, 
and the heat of the “fight or flight” or 
life passions. THE ORGAN FUNCTION MYSTERY The Triple Warmer Meridian is a 
somewhat mysterious Meridian in that 
it is said to have Organ Function in 
the Chinese energy system, yet it is 
not associated with a specific organ. It 
is said to have function, but no form, 
or it is alternatively defined as having 
its functions through the interactions 
of three different groups of organs 
found in the thoracic, upper and lower 
abdominal areas. The Function is associated with various 
interactions of gland secretions involved 
in our various human passions. The 
adrenal glands function in concert 
with other hormonal glands, partic-
ularly in fighting or running away 
in a moment of crisis, or recovery 
from injury, illness, malfunc-
tion and generalized stress. Four of the muscles that 
are related to this 
meridian func-
tion are muscles 
involved in run-
ning, pushing off, or getting on 
your toes, all essential fight or flight/
passion muscles. The pituitary 
gland works in concert with other 
endocrine glands that have functions 
related to the heat of life passions, 
procreative activity and sexual plea-
sure, life and death circumstances, 
causes worth suffering and dying 
for, etc. ELEMENT METAPHOR The Fire Element can be represented 
by the image of the hot, red sun, giving 
both light and warmth. It is associated 
with all of life’s heat and passions, par-
ticularly with enthusiasm, vitality and 
“warm feelings.” The Wood Element 
provides the fuel for Fire and the Earth 
Element is formed from the ashes gener-
ated by Fire. The Metal Element is con-
trolled by the intense heat of Fire which 
melts, purifies, and transforms Metal by 
forging it into a new and sturdy shape. Water controls Fire by tempering or 
quenching it.

excess energy, burning those around you, or 
are you too cold, unable to be passionate? Climate

you adapt to pressure and crisis with adequate counter-balancing measures? Odor

passions, demands, or  criticisms of others? Do you take risks, even at the cost 	
	
	
of getting scorched? E	 Do you have enough “fire in the belly”

emotions by “laughing them off”? Have you laughed at the wrong moment? Have you been made fun of, laughed at, scorned or ridiculed? Fortifies

expect precise reciprocity from others? E	 What are you fighting? E	 What paralyzes you? E	 Do you feel that your life is one of constant 
fight or flight? Are you always on the run? E	 What are you willing to suffer or even to 
die for?

5 10 Triple Warmer Meridian Teres Minor MUSCLE FUNCTION
This shoulder muscle rotates the arm and 
forearm. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Teres Minor INDICATIONS 
Teres Minor can be involved with shoulder 
problems. With weakness on one side, the 
hands will be turned differently when the 
arms are allowed to hang down at the sides. Thyroid conditions, digestive disturbances, 
infections, weight changes, and crying for 
no apparent reason may be associated with 
Teres Minor imbalance. This muscle is associ-
ated with the 3 “heats” of the Triple Warmer 
Meridian, particularly the heat of metabolism 
associated with thyroid function. The thyroid 
function involves the balance of metabolism 
in breaking down or regenerating tissues as 
well as maintaining body temperature and 
fat content. 10 T2 SITTING / STANDING / LYING FACE UP 1. Bend the elbow to 90˚ and hold it about a fist width to the side of 
the body, thumb towards shoulder. 2. Rotate forearm out as far as comfortable. Brace at front of elbow. 3. Pressure is against back of forearm, just above the wrist to push 
the forearm across the chest. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Against back of forearm above wrist. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Supplemental iodine substance or organic 
iodine, as found in such foods as seaweed 
(kelp) and raw or slightly cooked fish, espe-
cially if the person cries easily and often. P NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS P o On the temple at the 
hairline, slightly above and 
to the front of the ear. See 
figure on facing page. Hold 
this  point simultaneously 
with three fingers in a 
triangle in the depression just above the Sternum 
(breastbone). Test Also:  Check the Trapezius and Rhomboids for weakness if the Teres Minor appears to be weak.

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H
184 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Triple Warmer Meridian Sartorius MUSCLE FUNCTION
This is the longest muscle in the body. It 
serves to flex the leg and thigh, turn the 
thigh sideways and to flex the pelvis and/
or twist the hips. Called the “tailor’s mus-
cle” as it brings one leg over the opposite 
knee to create a lap for working on a piece 
of clothing. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Sartorius T11 STANDING / LYING FACE UP 1. With the leg turned out, knee slightly bent, and the foot 
brought up over the other leg, just below the knee. 2. Pressure is against the heel and the outside of the knee to 
straighten the leg. INDICATIONS 
Weakness will cause the pelvis to twist, and 
it can also be the source of knee pain or 
knock-knees because of the resulting insta-
bility of the knee joint. Adrenal problems 
which can affect this muscle are extreme 
fatigue in the morning which improves 
as the day goes on, blood pressure that 
does not rise when standing up, hypo-
glycemia (low blood sugar), hyper-insulin-
ism allergies, hives, and especially asthma. Infections will also take their toll, as will 
adrenal exhaustion. Pain in the Sartorius 
just above the knee can be an indication 
that a chiropractic adjustment is needed. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Against the heel and outside knee. P
P P FOODS FOR BALANCING
Adrenal substance and foods with high 
vitamin C content such as citrus and green 
peppers. P o NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS Test Also:  Adductors, Neck Muscles, Pectoralis Major Clavicular, Soleus, Gracilis, Gastrocnemius, 
Latissimus Dorsi. If swelling of the hands and feet is present, there may be a water imbalance, and the 
Psoas, Sacrospinalis, Peroneus  and Anterior Tibials should be checked. Posterior Fontanel, the 
soft spot on the back of 
the head.
section: 2. Rotate forearm out as far as comfortable.  Brace at front of elbow.

MUSCLE FUNCTION
This thigh muscle works with the Sartorius 
and Hamstrings. When lying face down, 
it is the first muscle used in bending the

gies may be related to the Gracilis. 2. Brace opposite leg. 3. Pressure is on the inside of the lower leg to pull the leg out to the 
side. PRESSURE

content such as citrus and green pepper. ALTERNATE TEST: STANDING / LYING FACE DOWN 1. With the knee bent about 45º. 2. Brace outside of knee (standing) and thigh on the same leg (prone). 3. Gentle pressure is against the inside of the lower leg to push the 
foot down and out to the side. This is a short range of movement 
only as the knee is not usually pushed in this direction. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Against inside lower leg. P

of the baby’s head. Test Also:  Deltoids, Coracobrachialis, and Diaphragm

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H
188 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Triple Warmer Meridian Soleus MUSCLE FUNCTION
This calf muscle flexes the foot and the 
lower part of the leg, stabilizing the foot. The Soleus works synergistically with the 
Gastrocnemius and the Plantaris, having in 
common the Achilles tendon and the Triceps 
Surae. The tendonous attachment above the 
knee of all three of these muscles, flexes 
the foot and the calf, thereby stabilizing 
the foot. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Soleus T11
T12 STANDING / LYING FACE DOWN 1. Bend leg 90˚ at knee, brace knee, and point the toes. When 
standing, a chair may be helpful. 2. Pressure is to push down the lower leg to straighten it. 3. Alternatively, pressure is against the back of the heel and the 
sole of the foot to straighten the foot. INDICATIONS 
Weakness may cause a forward lean of 
the body or the leg with the knee bent. Because of its relation to the adrenal glands, 
it is important to check this in cases of 
allergy, asthma, hypoglycemia, tiredness 
and fatigue. Emotional strain and the mild 
shock conditions from accidents or trauma, 
following the heavy outpouring of adren-
alin with excitement or pain can also be 
involved. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Downward against ball of foot. P FOODS FOR BALANCING
Foods rich in vitamin C (green pepper, 
citrus) are important. Allergy to tobacco 
may show up here. P o NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS Test Also:  Sartorius, Gracilis, Gastrocnemius, Pectoralis Major Clavicular, Neck Muscles, Latissimus 
Dorsi, Triceps, and Adductors. Posterior Fontanel, the 
soft spot on the back 
of the baby’s head.

text:
Touch for Health 
Letters from Around the World! How I Came To Touch for Health In 1975, I attended a conference in San 
Diego, where I met Dr. John F. Thie. My 
life changed! My medical practice changed. I changed on the personal level – how I 
interacted with friends, family – even my 
eating habits and the balance between work 
and leisure time changed. TFH is Instantaneous When I first encountered Kinesiology in 1988 
as a patient, I was intrigued by its simple 
examination system of testing indicator muscles 
to find dysfunction and then determining the 
best approach to take. The instant improvement 
of my health problem was impressive and led me 
to learn TFH. I began to study oriental medicine and 
encountered the view that the body is a 
system of energies. One day I had a patient 
who had a painful shoulder that she could 
not move. She was also very pale and suffered 
from profuse sweating. My entire medical 
know-how, including cortisone injections, 
caused no change in her condition. I 
prescribed several different drugs, an arm 
sling and more. I finally decided to apply 
the TFH techniques, which I did for 20 
minutes. Imagine my delight when she moved 
her arms higher and higher! In 1990, I started to use indicator muscle 
testing in my own practice on patients for 
whom traditional investigations did not provide 
any healing. The indicator muscle tests, in 
conjunction with the healing techniques, have 
turned out to be great assets in helping many of 
my patients in a non-invasive way. I began practicing Touch for Health from time 
to time in my practice, on those occasions 
when my medical school know-how was not 
getting results. For me it is very gratifying 
to do this work, pass it on to other people 
and then watch how they work with it. I am grateful to John Thie DC that he made 
these safe methods of health care widely known. The TFH system empowers people to help each 
other, without harming anyone or endangering our 
planet. Dr. Anna E Rolfes 
MBBS,MD,PhD 
Australia Warren Jacobs,MD
Escondido, CA

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H 
193
E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H Gallbladder Meridian The Gallbladder Meridian 
is associated with the small organ 
function of the Gallbladder that stores and concentrates the bile from the liver, 
which aids in digestion, particularly 
of fats. It contracts and releases bile 
when fats reach the duodenum from the 
stomach. On a cellular level this same 
function of maintaining fat metabolism 
relates to the subtle energy of the 
Gallbladder Meridian. ELEMENT METAPHOR The Wood Element metaphor is sym-
bolized by green growing natural forms 
of life. The Wood Element is fed by 
and springs from the Water Element, 
which can be thought of in terms of 
water feeding the roots of a tree, or 
even the original birth of life in the 
ocean. The Wood Element provides fuel 
for the Fire Element, nourishing its 
growth. The Wood Element controls 
the Earth Element by putting roots into 
the ground and holding it in place. The Wood Element is controlled by the 
Metal Element because of the powerful 
force Metal contains to chop, destroy or 
transform.

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H
194 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5
Gallbladder Meridian PEAK FUNCTION:  11 PM-1 AM 5-ELEMENT METAPHORS ENERGY FLOW:  YANG ELEMENT METAPHOR: WOOD Color	
Green:  Is there enough new growth in your life, or are you giving birth to more 	
	
	
new ideas, projects, etc. than can be sustained? E	 Do you have enough roots to sustain your 
growth, and enough fuel to sustain your pas-
sions? Season	
Spring:  What in your life needs to be revived, renewed, or reborn? What seeds 	
	
	
do you need to plant? E	 How does the image of green growing 
things relate to your life and your goals? Climate	
Wind:  Do you need “a breath of fresh air,” a “Spring cleaning” or are you 	 	
	
	
being blown away by uncertainty and change? E	 Do you have enough structure in your 
growth so that you can achieve your pur-
poses or do you need to allow yourself 
more freedom in your growth to be ful-
filled? Odor	
Rancid:  What do you need to let go of that was useful, but is now rancid? Do 	
	
	
you feel stagnant physically, emotionally, mentally or spiritually? E	 Do you need more freedom? Taste	
Sour: Has something that was good gone bad? Do you need to make  lemons 	
	
	
into lemonade? RELATED MUSCLES
	
Anterior Deltoid      
	
Popliteus Emotion	
Anger: Is your anger directed appropriately (yourself, a person, a thing)? Can 	
	
	
you feel outrage, or passions in general? Sound	
Shouting:  Do you need to shout/voice  your feelings and ideas? Are you 	
	
	
	
“blowing hot air,” out of self-importance, fear or insecurity? QUESTIONS TO CONSIDER
GALLBLADDER MERIDIAN 
METAPHORS Fortifies	
Ligaments: Do you take precautions? Do you need to “push the envelope”? Running on reserves or secondary systems? E	 How are you not digesting the heavy parts 
of your life functioning? Personal Power	
Birth:  What do you need to let out, to give birth to? Need a fresh start? Starting too many new things? E	 Are you not diluting your life enough with 
clear water that is bringing in the neutral 
parts to reduce the concentration of dis-
solving or breaking aspects of your life? Faith/Worldview	
Infancy/Early Childhood or Intuitive-Projective Faith:  Is there only one 	 	
	
	
correct  viewpoint? Do you use personal intuition, and dream imagery to find 	
	
	
meaning? Are you dwelling in your own dream-world, ignoring cause and effect, 	
	
	
assuming that what seems right to you will be accepted by others? E	 Do you remain too concentrated for your own 
good?

5 11 Gallbladder	 Meridian Anterior Deltoid MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS
MUSCLE FUNCTION
Along with the Coracobrachialis, helps 
flex the shoulder with the elbow bent, as 
in combing the hair. Anterior Deltoid 11 T4 SITTING / STANDING / LYING FACE UP INDICATIONS 
It is particularly associated with the 
Gallbladder Meridian Function of concen-
trating/releasing bile to aid in digestion 
of heavy, dense substances such as fats. Inhibition in this muscle is sometimes 
associated with headaches related to tox-
icity from dietary indiscretions or eating 
fatty foods. 1. Position straight arm slightly less than 45˚ in front of the body and 
slightly over the outside of the thigh, palm facing down. 2. Brace on the top of the shoulder as needed. 3. Pressure is against the forearm above the wrist to push the arm 
down. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Downward against the forearm above    
the wrist. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Foods rich in vitamin A such as carrots, 
parsley and other yellow and green veg-
etables. Avoid fats and grilled or fried 
foods, pastries, rich sweets, pork. P
P o NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS Anterior Fontanel, the 
soft spot on the top of 
the baby’s head. Test Also:  Coracobrachialis, Popliteus, Deltoids, and Pectoralis Major Sternal.

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H
198 E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 Gallbladder	 Meridian Popliteus MUSCLE FUNCTION
This muscle turns the foot and knee in and 
flexes the leg. MUSCLE TEST POSITIONS Popliteus INDICATIONS 
Many knee pains can be associated with 
Popliteus weakness and it is sometimes asso-
ciated with neck pains. There may be hyper-
extension of the knee (knee pushed too far 
back), and bending the knee may become 
difficult or painful. Gallbladder conditions, 
jaundice, shingles, one-sided headaches, con-
stipation and feeling sleepy after eating fats 
may be related to this. Sometimes the weak-
ness may come from the lower three neck 
bones being “fixed” or stuck together. T12 SITTING / STANDING / LYING FACE UP 1. Create  right angles at the ankle and knee and fully rotate the lower leg 
and foot inwards. While standing, a chair for support may be helpful. 2. Brace the heel. Pressure is against the inside of the foot to rotate 
outwards. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P
Against inside of the foot. ALTERNATE TEST: LYING FACE UP 1. With leg bent and dropped out to the side, stabilize at ankle. 2. Pressure is against the outside of the knee, to twist the lower leg in 
relation to the thigh. If the Popliteus is strong, this torque will be felt 
in the hip muscles. Be aware of the muscle at the back of the knee. FOODS FOR BALANCING
Foods rich in vitamin A, such as green leafy 
and yellow vegetables, and liver are helpful. Avoid: fats, rich sweets, and fried foods. Against the outside of the knee, to twist 
the lower leg in relation to the thigh. PRESSURE 
TARGET
P CAUTION:  
With all knee problems, 
this muscle should be 
carefully evaluated, 
especially with repeated 
testing which may 
indicate a need for pro-
longed Neurolymphatic 
massage. Exercising 
the neck gently may 
help, and holding lightly 
on the point where  
the collarbone meets 
the breastbone, but 
chiropractic adjustment 
may be necessary if 
the Popliteus does not 
respond to balancing. NEUROVASCULAR 
HOLDING POINTS
Hold the center 
of the back of the 
knee simultaneously 
with the origin of 
Popliteus on the 
outside of the knee, 
just below and 
slightly toward the back of the knee. Then hold 
the center at the same time as the insertion, at 
the inside of the leg. Also, the Sternoclavicular 
Joint, where the collarbone meets the 
breastbone. (See facing page.) P First
o
o P o Second Test Also:  Anterior Deltoid, Quadriceps, and Pectoralis Major Sternal.
section: 1. With leg bent and dropped out to the side, stabilize at ankle.

text:
Touch for Health 
Letters from Around the World! My next idea was to finally become a Touch 
For Health Instructor and I took my training 
at Vancouver, Canada. Touch For Health for Me! Now I am a Holistic Health Practitioner. I 
use TFH on myself, in my practice,  and I 
also teach it at a bodywork institute and out 
of my home. My vocation has shifted from being a 
Management Analyst in Human Resouces for 
the County of Santa Clara to the holistic 
practice and teaching Touch For Health. The 
last few years I have been a member of the 
Board of Directors of the Touch For Health 
Association of America. In l979, I was a wife with one child, working 
full time for the County of Santa Clara as an 
Eligibility Worker and going to college in the 
evening. Although I had a busy life, I was interested 
in health related issues. My general health 
was very good. I did have a “pigeon toe” i.e. 
a turned in right foot which I had had since I 
could remember. I saw a Touch For Health (TFH) Class listed in 
an Adult School catalog taught by Cliff Garner. I decided to find out what it was about. I took 
all three levels from Cliff. In l985, I had 
the opportunity to go to Valais, Switzerland 
for a Touch For Health Conference. My husband 
was going on a business trip to Europe and 
asked me if there wasn’t some conference in 
Europe I could attend so we could “write off” 
the trip expenses. I remembered a flyer which 
I received in the mail and didn’t remember 
what modality the conference was about. The 
flyer was for a Touch For Health Conference 
in Valais, Switzerland. It just seemed that 
Touch For Health was tapping me on the shoulder 
to recognize it. It was at that conference in 
Switzerland that I first saw John Thie, and met 
the faculty and many wonderful folks who are 
still a part of the TFH Community. Touch For Health has changed the way I 
think about Wellness. I believe we have some 
control over our health and wellbeing. It has 
changed the work I do and the way I approach 
my clients. Many thanks to John Thie for 
making this wonderful work available to folks 
like me. Yvonne Bowman
Board of Directors, TFH Association of America
Certified Touch For Health Instructor Just before the Conference, some folks from 
Norway gave me a balance and my “pigeon toe” 
was corrected. (switched off psoas on the 
right side). This seemed miraculous since the 
Medical community thought I was too old for any 
corrective intervention.

text:
T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H 
201
E L E M E N T
METAPHOR
5 T O U C H  F O R  H E A L T H Liver Meridian The Liver Meridian is associ-
ated with the liver which has more known 
functions than any other organ. Each cell 
has more programmed instructions than 
the most sophisticated computer pro-
gram, and the Liver Meridian may acti-
vate a large number of the absorption and 
detoxification instructions. The liver is the largest physical organ and is most active 
while you sleep. The liver is said to store 
blood while the body is at rest, and also to 
exert particular influence over the lower 
Abdomen, and so is considered of cen-
tral importance in women’s menstrual 
cycle and sexuality. Liver Meridian 
Function is involved in digestion, metab-
olism, storage and distribution of nour-
ishment, filtration, detoxification, and 
immune function. ELEMENT METAPHOR The Wood Element metaphor is sym-
bolized by green growing natural forms 
of life. The Wood Element is fed by 
and springs from the Water Element, 
which can be thought of in terms of 
water feeding the roots of a tree, or 
even the original birth of life in the 
ocean. The Wood Element provides fuel 
for the Fire Element, nourishing its 
growth. The Wood Element controls 
the Earth Element by putting roots into 
the ground and holding it in place. The Wood Element is controlled by the 
Metal Element because of the powerful 
force Metal contains to chop, destroy or 
transform.
